title: Delissea procera
intro: Delissea procera, also known as Hawaiian holly, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. Traditionally, Delissea procera is used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Delissea procera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Delissea procera is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Delissea procera is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes over 2,300 species found in many parts of the world. Delissea procera is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is an endemic species of the region.
